item.title: Spector Euro 4 LT Bass Guitar - Red Fade Gloss — $2,999.99
item.description:
$2,999.99 · Brand New · PRO DG AMERICA

Handcrafted with impeccable attention to detail, the Spector Euro4 LT 4-string bass guitar is an instrument you’ll treasure for a lifetime. Featuring the acclaimed design of Spector’s Euro series, the Euro4 LT’s contoured body has been carefully weight relieved for an even more comfortable playing experience. For world-class tone, you have a pair of custom-wound Bartolini pickups, shaped through a custom-voiced Darkglass active tone circuit. Complete with Gotoh lightweight tuners and Spector’s innovative locking bridge, the Spector Euro4 LT electric bass will make anyone feel like a top-tier bass player. The Spector Euro4 LT comes loaded with a set of custom-wound Bartolini pickups. Built exclusively for Spector, these hum-canceling pickups deliver an open, resonant sound and unmatched note articulation. This classic P/J pairing features a single-conductor shielded cable that minimizes unwanted interference. The wide range of tones that these pickups exhibit is well-suited for any playing style. And for powerful tone-shaping capabilities, the Euro4 LT features a Darkglass active preamp and tone circuit. Spector (with the help of Ned Steinberger) developed the contoured NS body design in 1977, prioritizing the player’s comfort for possibly the first time in the history of the bass guitar. And players soon took note, with iconic bassists such as Garry Tallent, Ian Hill, Rudy Sarzo, and countless others taking to the stage with Spector basses. This iconic body style balances well and feels supremely comfortable against your body and arm. And thanks to the Euro4 LT’s carefully tuned weight relief, you’ll enjoy a lighter instrument without making any sonic sacrifices. If you’ve always craved a Spector NS bass, you’ll dig the Euro Series. These basses are essentially production versions of those coveted instruments, with the same sleek carved bodies as their US-made cousins. Euro Series basses were handcrafted in the Czech Republic using top-shelf tonewoods from the United States and Europe — you’ll love the natural resonance and tone of these instruments. What’s more, you get graphite-reinforced truss rods, premium pickups, and the acclaimed Darkglass active preamp. The Euro Series continues Stuart Spector’s decades-long tradition of innovation. Features:
  • 4-string production version of the legendary Spector NS bass
  • 3-piece maple neck-through design delivers snap, speed, and sustain
  • Darkglass active preamp system with bass and treble controls, internal mid-control trimpot, and bridge and middle volume controls
  • European alder body with weight relief that your shoulder will appreciate
  • Custom Bartolini pickups deliver high output with exceptional punch and definition

item.title: Spector Euro 4 RST Bass Guitar - Sienna Stain — $3,099.99
item.description:
$3,099.99 · Brand New · PRO DG AMERICA

Spector continues its tradition of forging sleek, ultramodern basses with the Euro RST 4-string bass. The Euro RST's sculpted body is built from a richly figured flame maple top and a welterweight empresswood back. Together with its 3-piece roasted maple through-neck, the Euro RST delivers big-time tone and feel along with stability to survive and thrive on the road. A soft satin finish from head to toe causes the figuring in these woods to practically glow under lights. Under the hood, the Spector Euro RST's advanced electronics give you the control to sculpt your tone for stage and studio. A set of Aguilar Super Double soapbar pickups, fed by an Aguilar OBP-2 preamp, gives you crushing lows, cutting mids, and a sparkling top end to master any style of music presented before you. A neck built from three pieces of roasted maple — so called for its moisture-zapping heat treatment and so built for its incredible stability — delivers a tonally and visually distinctive playing experience in the Spector Euro RST bass guitar from Sweetwater. The Euro RST neck-through construction delivers gut-rumbling sustain and the type of stability touring bassists could set their watch to. The Euro RST's sculpted signature Euro body is comprised of a highly figured flame maple top and a lightweight empresswood back. These woods more than give this bass a distinctive tone. They're also enriched with a soft matte finish to cause the natural figuring to practically glow under stage lights. Aguilar's Super Double bass pickups offer up a thick, growling tone in the Spector Euro RST while never losing the nuance and articulation you demand. Their exceptional string-to-string balance has made them a favorite among gigging bass players at Sweetwater. These Super Double pickups offer incredible richness and depth, all while canceling the troublesome hum that can plague single-coil designs. An onboard Aguilar OBP-2 active preamp with +18dB of added 40Hz thump and +16dB of tasty 6.5kHz twang tailors your Euro RST to just about any musical setting, from jazz to metal. If you’ve always craved a Spector NS bass, but found them to be out of your budget, you’ll dig the Euro Series. These basses are essentially production versions of those coveted instruments, with the same sleek carved bodies as their US-made cousins. Euro Series basses were handcrafted in the Czech Republic using top-shelf tonewoods from the United States and Europe — you’ll love the natural resonance and tone of these instruments. What’s more, you get graphite-reinforced truss rods, premium pickups, and powerful active preamps. The Euro Series continues Stuart Spector’s decades-long tradition of innovation. Features:
  • The latest from Spector's ultra-modern Euro Series
  • Sculpted body made from richly figured flame maple and lightweight empresswood
  • Soft satin finish causes woods to practically glow under lights
  • Durable neck-through construction
  • Roasted maple neck delivers a tonally and visually distinctive playing experience
  • Hum-canceling Aguilar Super Double pickups offer up thick tone with plenty of top-end articulation
  • Dual rows of Alnico V magnets provide outstanding balance across all strings
  • OBP-2 preamp lets you paint with a broad palette of colors
UPC:838164006619

item.title: Dingwall ABZ 4 Custom Shop Natural Swamp Ash Bass Guitar — $2,499
item.description:
$2,499 · Excellent · Philip's Bass Garage

Description

Up for sale is a beautiful, Canadian custom-built Dingwall ABZ 4-string bass. Known for their incredible clarity, perfect string tension, and unparalleled ergonomic design, this handmade instrument from Saskatoon is an absolute player's dream.

This bass features a stunning two-piece swamp ash body with a striking natural grain, paired with a rock-solid maple neck and a lightning-fast maple fretboard. It is entirely passive, delivering a pure, punchy, and incredibly organic tone that sits perfectly in any mix.

Upgrades & Hardware

Tuners: Upgraded at the factory with premium black Hipshot hardware.

Drop Tuning: Includes a factory-installed Hipshot USA Ultralight Bass Xtender (D-Tuner) on the low E string for instantaneous drop tuning with flawless pitch accuracy.

Feel & Playability

Weighing in at a highly comfortable ~8 lbs, this bass is exceptionally well-balanced and light on the shoulder for long gigs. The neck profile is incredibly ergonomic—it isn’t thin or fragile-feeling; rather, it has a comfortable depth that fills the hand beautifully and naturally without feeling bulky, even with a comfortably narrow nut width. The FD3 pickups sound a little bit smoother than those on my NG3, likely due to the placement.

Condition

I would comfortably classify this bass in Excellent Condition. It has been lovingly played and meticulously maintained over the years. There are a few light cosmetic markings and subtle "smoother spots" in the matte finish from honest play wear, which I have done my best to capture clearly in the photos.

Technical Specifications

  • Model: ABZ (4-String)
  • Origin: Handmade in Canada
  • Body Material: 2-Piece Swamp Ash
  • Finish: Matte/Satin Natural
  • Neck Material: Maple
  • Fingerboard: Maple (Multiscale fanned-fret layout)
  • Scale Length: Multiscale (36.25" to 34")
  • Electronics: Completely Passive with Dingwall FD3 pickups
  • Controls: Volume, Rotary Pickup Selector, Tone
  • Hardware: Black bridge, Hipshot Tuners + Bass Xtender
  • Weight: Approx. 8 lbs

item.title: Kala Solidbody U-Bass - Compact Electric Bass - Orange Paisley - w/Gig Bag — $449
item.description:
$449 · Brand New · Cumberland Guitars

For the musician who wants their gear to look as loud as their performance, the Paisley Orange Burst U•BASS® fuses a vibrant vintage-inspired graphic with a modern passive electronics designed for any stage. The solid okoume body and maple neck and fretboard (a first for the solid body U•BASS®) pair up for a clear attack and generous sustain, powered by a magnetic split-coil pickup that handles everything from smooth jazz to heavy rock. It is the go-to tool for performers who want to inject some personality into their portable setup without sacrificing professional tone.

This bass includes a nice padded gig bag.

Optional salt-and-pepper tweed hardshell case with red velvet interior is shown.

Show in the photos is the other color offered by Kala: Floral Black Burst.

This bass is available for a test drive in our Jamestown, Kentucky showroom, and we ship worldwide. Let us know if you have any questions!

Weight: 5lbs 3oz

item.title: Sire Marcus Miller V7 4-String Bass - Natural (Swamp Ash / Maple) — $600
item.description:
$600 · Very Good · TrueTone Exchange

Up for sale is a fantastic Sire Marcus Miller V7 4-String Bass featuring a stunning natural Swamp Ash body, a gorgeous maple neck/fretboard, and an eye-catching pearloid pickguard. This is a highly sought-after 1st Generation model, easily identifiable by the beautiful matching genuine ash wood battery compartment cover on the back.

This bass is famous for outperforming instruments twice its price range, combining classic vintage Jazz bass aesthetics with a incredibly versatile modern preamp system.

Key Features & Specs:

  • Body: Solid Swamp Ash (Natural finish with excellent grain patterns)
  • Neck & Fretboard: Hard Maple neck with white binding and pearloid block inlays
  • Nut Width: 1.5" (Classic slim Jazz profile)
  • Scale Length: 34" standard long scale
  • Pickups: Marcus Super-J Revolution set
  • Electronics: Marcus Miller Heritage-3 18V Active/Passive Preamp (gives you an incredible range of tonal shaping from classic 70s slap to modern thud).
  • Strings-Through-Body: Offers fantastic sustain and resonance.

Condition:

The bass is in excellent functional and very good cosmetic condition. The neck is straight, the frets show minimal wear, and the 18V electronics work perfectly with zero scratchy pots.

Shipping & Packaging:

Will be securely packed inside a heavy-duty, standard 52" bass shipping box with professional packing materials to ensure it arrives safely.

item.title: Sire Marcus Miller M6 5-string Headless Left-handed Bass Guitar - Tobacco Sunburst Satin — $750
item.description:
$750 · Excellent · Michael's Lefty Guitars and Basses

Purchased new in early April 2026, it is in mint condition - no blemishes or issues, set up last week again with new strings and adjusted action. .40s on the bass now. All hex wrenches and original tuners included.

NOTE: The original tuners were impossible to turn with fingers as they were too smooth and slippery. Sire realized this and offered replacement tuners with better grip to any verified buyers. I put those on immediately as tuning with a hex wrench was awful. The new tuners are good - you can actually use them and turn them! Check out some reviews on YouTube and you will see a lot of talk about the original tuners. But for what it is worth they are included.


Comes with original Sire Premium gig bag - an excellent quality bag that was a nice unexpected bonus.


The bass is lightweight, sounds great, high quality construction and beautiful inlays, so why sell it? I must be too old but I cannot adjust to the headless thing, or the fanned fret thing. I have tried three times, it's just not for me! Maybe it is right for you! Aesthetically this is a beautiful instrument and I hate to sell it for that reason but I just like my comfort zone heavy basses with 4 strings!

item.title: Brubaker JXB-USA-5 JXB-5 jxb5 5-string Bass with Bag 2025 - Silver Steel Satin — $2,199
item.description:
$2,199 · Very Good · Atomic Music

This is a pre-owned/used instrument available as shown for local delivery, pickup at our storefront (curbside or in store) in Beltsville, Maryland, or shipment to those in the lower 48/USA only via UPS Ground [a signature will be required for delivery]*.

  • Its electronics have been tested and function 100% as they should.
  • See the provided pix for best idea of overall cosmetic condition. As it is pre-owned, it has likely acquired surface marks and wear (dings, dents, scratches, scuffs, smudges, etc.) from play/use - all purely cosmetic in nature. There are no issues to note with regard to functionality and playability.
  • Its COA and the pictured gig bag will be included.  No other extras are available for this item.
  • This listing has been created for the benefit of our local customers.  There may be cosmetic or functional issues that the pictures alone cannot capture — if you are not able to come by and inspect this instrument in person, please be sure to ask any relevant questions you may have regarding its condition, playability, etc. before buying.

* Please be aware that humidity, temperature, and handling can affect guitars & basses such that adjustments to the instrument might be needed post-shipment. This includes "fret sprout" (as wood can shrink/swell during shipment, a fretted instrument may arrive with sharp fret edges). If tweaking action, intonation, etc., having fret edges dressed, or having the instrument set up to your specs is not something you are comfortable with doing yourself, you will need the help of a local-to-you tech/professional. Note that any associated fees incurred for their time and labor will be your responsibility.

Please contact us to discuss any other questions you may have about it before clicking the "Add To Cart" button!


Atomic Music store hours:  12pm - 6pm, EST, Monday - Saturday & 12pm - 5pm, EST, Sundays.

Thanks!!

- Atomic Music

item.title: Hofner Ignition Violin Bass Left Handed (HIBBLSB) - Sunburst (Inc Gig Bag) — $380.01
item.description:
$380.01 · Excellent · Cookes Band Instruments

Pre Loved Hofner Ignition HI-BB Violin Bass Left Handed Inc Bag

In an excellent used condition - Light marks from minimal use and cleaning - slight tarnishing in places on the Chrome hardware. The bass comes with a Deluxe Fender padded gig bag which fits very well!

By Hofner:

Inspired by Hofner's iconic 1970s Violin Bass, the Hofner Ignition Violin Bass delivers the classic vintage look, feel, and unmistakable Hofner tone at an affordable price. Unlike the HCT model, the Ignition features a fully hollow body for authentic resonance, along with the legendary Hofner control panel, traditional wooden bridge, and classic Hofner-style tailpiece.

Perfect for players seeking the famous Hofner sound without the premium price tag, the Ignition Violin Bass combines lightweight comfort with warm, punchy tones that have made this design a favourite for decades. This left-handed model is available exclusively in a stunning Sunburst finish.

Specifications:

  • Finish: Sunburst
  • Top: Spruce
  • Back & Sides: Flame Maple
  • Body: Fully Hollow (No Centre Block)
  • Neck: Maple/Beech/Maple
  • Fingerboard: Rosewood
  • Scale Length: 30" (76cm)
  • Nut Width: 42mm
  • Frets: 22
  • Neck Pickup: Hofner Ignition Staple Nickel
  • Bridge Pickup: Hofner Ignition Staple Nickel

item.title: Bacchus WL-433/R BLK — $414.84
item.description:
$414.84 · Good · Astronauts Guitars

Bacchus JB-type WOODLINE series

While standard JB-types generally use a 34-inch scale, this WL-433 uses a 33-inch scale. Accordingly, the body size is also slightly more compact, resulting in an easy-to-handle instrument.

The controls are the same as a standard JB-type 2 volumes and 1 tone.
Plus, the two pickups will be connected in series by pulling up the tone knob, allowing you to enjoy a thick, powerful sound besides the usual JB sound.

There are minor scratches, but no noticeable damage, and it is in generally good condition overall.
The neck and frets are in good condition, and playability is good.

Because the scale is shorter, string tension is also a little softer, making it recommended for a. player with smaller hands or shorter stature.
It is also easy to handle as a first instrument, with less strain even during long playing sessions!


Specs

– Scale: 33-inch scale (approx. 838 mm)

– Number of frets: 21

– Weight: 4.3 kg

– Accessories: general-purpose soft case


Condition

– No parts have been replaced.

– There are small dents and scratches, but no noticeable damage.

– The neck is straight, with no warping or twisting.

– The frets have no major wear and are 90% or more remaining.

– The truss rod works properly in both directions.


Setup
– Strung with D'Addario 45-105 strings.
– Action at the 12th fret: 1st string 2 mm, 4th string 2.5 mm


Please note

- We are unable to accept price negotiations, as the listed price already reflects our best.

- The shipping fees listed on the item page are reference prices and may differ from the actual shipping cost (including Reverb and PayPal fees.) While the amounts are accurate for many countries, they may vary significantly for others.

- All customs taxes and VAT are the buyers’ responsibility. Please check out the requirements for your country.

- All items are also for sale in our physical workshop in Takadanobaba. There is a chance they might be sold out.

- We sell only at Reverb for customers from outside Japan.

- We accept payment only through Reverb platform.

item.title: Fender American Vintage II '66 Jazz Bass 2023 - 3-Color Sunburst — $2,259.34
item.description:
$2,259.34 · Excellent · Bass Direct

The Fender® American Vintage II series presents a remarkably accurate take on the revolutionary designs that altered the course of musical history. Built with period-accurate bodies, necks and hardware, premium finishes and meticulously voiced, year-specific pickups, each instrument captures the essence of authentic Fender craftsmanship and tone.

When Don Randall at Fender Sales requested a more sophisticated, high-end bass model, the now iconic Jazz Bass® was the result. The neck was narrower at the nut, providing a faster and more effortless feel and the sleek new body style mirrored the offset contoured style of the Jazzmaster®. By 1966, evolution of the instrument reached new heights with striking new features.

The American Vintage II 1966 Jazz Bass features a comfortable “U” shape hard rock maple neck, and it plays like a dream with a 7.25″ radius round-lam rosewood fingerboard and vintage tall frets. Characteristic of production in 1966, this J-Bass® is offered with white neck binding, pearl dots, two volume controls with master tone, matching painted headstock and 4-ply tortoiseshell pickguard. The 4-saddle bridge with threaded saddles, vintage-style “lollipop” tuners and Pure Vintage ’66 Jazz Bass pickups deliver classic thump and style.

The instruments in the American Vintage II series are direct descendants of the original Fenders: designed for players with a fine appreciation for vintage Fender tone and feel and built with unmatched quality, down to the last screw. These are Fender electrics in their purest form: Fender American Vintage II, the stuff of legends.

Presented in fantastic condition and supplied with original hardcase.

  • Serial Number: V2321079
  • Origin: USA
  • Year: 2023
  • Body Material: Alder
  • Colour: 3 tone sunburst
  • Finish: Nitro
  • Neck Finish: Nitro
  • Neck Material: Maple
  • Fingerboard: Rosewood
  • Inlays: Dots, side dots
  • Pickups: Fender

item.title: Fender Player II Precision Bass - Cactus Gray — $849.99
item.description:
$849.99 · Brand New · Haggerty's Music

-YOU ARE VIEWING PHOTOS OF THE ACTUAL BASS BEING SOLD!

This brand new 8.6 lb Fender will ship insured, fully set up, and inspected from Rapid City, SD.

Buy risk free with our 14 day - full refund, less shipping, return policy. Check out our shop policies for more details.

The Fender Player II Precision Bass carries forward one of the most recorded and recognizable bass designs ever made, blending classic Fender feel with thoughtful modern refinements. From the first note, it delivers the punch, authority, and unmistakable low-end presence that has anchored countless recordings across every genre. Whether you’re laying down a steady groove, driving a rock band, or sitting deep in a studio mix, this bass feels immediately familiar and inspiring.

The alder body provides a balanced, resonant foundation, while the Modern “C” maple neck offers a smooth, comfortable feel that encourages long playing sessions. Rolled fingerboard edges and a 9.5” radius add a broken-in feel right out of the case, making transitions up and down the neck effortless. It’s the kind of instrument that stays out of your way and lets you focus entirely on the music.

At the heart of the Player II Precision Bass is a Player Series Alnico V split-coil pickup that delivers everything players love about a great P Bass; thick lows, rich mids, and enough clarity to cut through any mix. The adjustable four-saddle bridge and open-gear tuners provide dependable tuning stability and solid sustain, making this a true workhorse for stage and studio alike. The Fender Player II Precision Bass offers timeless tone, modern comfort, and the versatility to handle whatever gig comes next. Available now at Haggerty’s Music.

Features / Specs

Highlights

  • Series: Player II
  • Orientation: Right-Handed
  • Color: Cactus Gray

Body

  • Body Material: Alder
  • Body Finish: Gloss Polyester
  • Body Shape: Precision Bass
  • Body Style: Solid

Neck

  • Headstock Type: Precision Bass
  • Neck Material: Maple
  • Neck Shape: Modern C
  • Neck Finish: Satin Urethane
  • Neck Depth: 0.828" at 1st Fret, 0.897" at 12th Fret
  • Neck Construction: 4-Bolt
  • Fingerboard Radius: 9.5"
  • Fingerboard Material: Maple
  • Position Inlays: Black Dot
  • Side Dots: Black
  • Number of Frets: 20
  • Truss Rod Style: Single Action
  • Truss Rod Nut: Standard

item.title: 【used】HEX Guitars / NB505 SB 3.790kg #HJ1202721【GIB Yokohama】【price included SHIPPING & IMPORT TAX & VAT】 — $918
item.description:
$918 · Very Good · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

HEX Guitars is a South Korea-based brand specializing in entry-level to mid-range guitars.


They own their own factory in Indonesia, and the company president himself lives there to maintain a high level of quality control.


This dedication, evident in the president's personal involvement rather than outsourcing, allows them to produce guitars of a quality far exceeding what one might expect from their price point.


The NB505 is the base model for their newly launched ""NEXT SERIES.""


It features modern and comprehensive specifications, including fanned frets and active circuitry.


The refined body shape is crafted from mahogany.


The neck utilizes a laminated design of maple and walnut.


By laminating several pieces of wood to form the neck, left-right asymmetry is minimized, resulting in high stability and robustness.


The fretboard is made of walnut, with a relatively flat 12-inch radius.


The neck shape is relatively thin, a slim C-shape.


The frets are made of stainless steel.


Stainless steel is characterized by its lack of tarnishing and high hardness.


Stainless steel frets are popular because they retain their shine indefinitely and are extremely resistant to wear.


They are also used by high-end guitar brands such as Suhr Guitars.


The pickups are original single-coil pickups.


They use original pickups in many of their models.


This is not for cost-cutting purposes, but because they have confidence in the quality of the pickups they design themselves.


The pickups are designed by staff who know music and play music on a daily basis, after thoroughly researching many elements such as magnets, wires, and bobbins.


It features an original preamp, allowing for flexible sound shaping with a 3-band EQ.


The EQ frequency range is practical: BASS 50Hz, MIDDLE 1kHz, Treble 6kHz.


The controls are Volume (active/passive switch with push-pull switch), Balancer, BASS, MIDDLE, and TREBLE. (Preamp gain can be adjusted from the back panel)


The balance in your hand is excellent, and even those who usually play Fenders will likely find the HEX NB easy to play.


No special headless strings are needed; standard bass strings (Jazz Bass or Precision Bass) can be used.


It has a clean sound typical of headless basses, yet it doesn't lose musical harmonics or rich bass tones.


The basic quality of the neck is very high, resulting in excellent playability; it's difficult to say it's inferior to instruments in the 200,000 yen range.


Overall, it's a superb product with high quality in looks, playability, and sound.


You might be hesitant because it's headless, but I highly recommend trying it out.


Fret life is approximately 90%.


The truss rod is in good condition.


The neck is in good condition.


The body and paintwork show some minor scratches, but overall it's clean.


The electronics are in good condition.

No obvious modifications were observed.


Controls: Volume (Push-Pull / Active-Passive), Balancer, Treble, Middle, Bass

Nut Width: 46.0mm

Bridge String Spacing: 18.0mm

Scale Length: 33-35 Inch

Year of Manufacture: Unknown

Country of Manufacture: INDONESIA


This instrument will be delivered after being adjusted for playability by a GIB craftsman.

Fender is, of course, the number one brand of electric guitars and basses.

The Precision Bass, the world's first fretted solid-body electric bass, instantly captivated bassists worldwide.

Its brilliance remains unchanged and it continues to be loved even more than half a century after its creation.

This model is a new Fender, restructured after the discontinuation of Fender Japan.

While manufactured in Japan, it incorporates even more American specifications and feedback than before.

This is a replica model that reproduces the specifications of the 1950s.

The body is made of basswood, and the neck is made of 1P maple.

Understanding that cost constraints prevent everything from being done exactly as desired, this choice is not bad.

The pickups are Vintage-Style Split Single-Coil Precision Bass.

The deep bass and light midrange are unmistakably Fender Precision.

It fits perfectly with all genres, including R&B, rock, and pop.

Music Man emerged as Leo Fender's second brand and became beloved by bassists worldwide.
Many artists, including Louis Johnson and Flea, are known to use their basses. (Titles omitted)

The StingRay is a classic model that enjoys overwhelming popularity within the brand.
It's no exaggeration to say that, alongside Fender's Jazz Bass and Precision Bass, it's one of the most popular basses in the world.

It features a 6-bolt neck joint and an oil-finished neck.

The unique sound, with its powerful, punchy lows and highs, can only be achieved with this bass.

This is a must-try instrument for anyone playing funk or nu-metal.

item.title: 1971 Fender Jazz Bass - Sunburst — $7,316.93
item.description:
$7,316.93 · Very Good · Andy Baxter Bass & Guitars Ltd

1971 Fender Jazz bass in very good condition. Sunburst finish, rosewood fingerboard, Tortoiseshell guard. Serial: 338993. Except for the missing metal pickup and bridge covers, this bass is all original and complete, including the case. 

This is a very nice example of a vintage Fender Jazz bass. The finish has a number of small scuffs and dings, mainly around the edges, as well as some buckle rash on the back.  It has a fantastic neck, which is clean overall with only light wear and tear. The frets are clean and have plenty of life in them, having been dressed in our workshop. The headstock is clean overall with just a little bit of scuffing along the top edge. A previous owner etched their name and Social Security number onto the metal backplate and the control plate.

This bass is a joy to play, with a superb, even sound right across the fretboard; it has a straight neck, and plenty of room in the truss rod. It's been fully set up in our workshop and is strung with Ernie Ball round-wound strings. Weight 9 lbs 2 oz / 4.14 kg. The neck date is Dec 71. The pots all date to the 16th week of 1971. The mute sponge is stuck to the underside of the pickups, so no dates are visible. Nut Width: 37.5 mm. Depth at 1st fret: 22.5 mm.

item.title: 【used】Bacchus / WL5-ASH/RSM STB Mod. 5.165kg #GI13388【GIB Yokohama】【price included SHIPPING & IMPORT TAX & VAT】 — $1,078
item.description:
$1,078 · Very Good · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

Bacchus is a Japanese brand based in Nagano.


It's a brand under the Deviser umbrella, which owns a large guitar factory called the Asuka Factory. The high quality, stemming from the factory's in-house production, is highly regarded by many musicians.


This model is the WL model, part of the WOODLINE series, Bacchus's top-of-the-line lineage.


The body is made of ash, and the neck and fretboard are made of roasted maple.


The most distinctive feature is the use of roasted maple for the neck and fretboard.


This is also known as thermowood.


Thermowood, which was commercialized in the musical instrument field by Sago of Japan, was originally used in Finland as a building material for saunas.


Thermowood is valued for its resistance to high temperatures and humidity, and its usefulness in musical instruments is evident from its adoption by top manufacturers such as Sadowsky and Suhr.


The neck pickup has been replaced with an EMG-LJ5AX, and the bridge pickup with an SJ5AX.


This is a modification aimed at achieving a clearer output.


This guitar features the brand's signature smooth playability, and the sound is well-balanced and smooth from low to high frequencies.


It delivers a powerful sound while maintaining excellent response.


It's a pleasure to play, and you'll want to play this instrument for hours.


This guitar fully showcases the potential of Roasted Maple; please give it a try.


The frets have approximately 80% remaining.


The truss rod is in good condition.


The neck is in good condition.


The body and paintwork show some minor scratches on the pickguard. There is a dent of about 5mm near the bridge pickup.


The electronics are in good condition.


The pickups have been replaced with EMG pickups. Accordingly, the pots, jack, and wiring have all been replaced. The string guides have been replaced with Hip Shot ones.


Controls: Neck pickup volume, bridge pickup volume, master tone

item.title: 【used】Warwick / RockBass Corvette Basic 5-String Bass Burgundy Red 2014 3.715kg #RB C 532762-14【GIB Yokohama】【price included SHIPPING & IMPORT TAX & VAT】 — $984
item.description:
$984 · Very Good · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

Warwick is a globally renowned guitar and bass brand based in Germany.

In recent years, they have begun distributing other world-class brands such as Sadowsky.

They have increased their presence in many fields and release numerous attractive products.

Many top artists, including Stuart Zender, Jonas Hellborg, and Robert Trujillo, use Warwick basses (titles omitted).

They have made their excellence known to the world.

While the company offers several models, this is the particularly high-end Corvette.

The body is made of alder and the fretboard is rosewood, which is a classic combination, but somewhat unusual for a Warwick product.

The neck is made of 3-piece laminated maple.

This technique of laminating multiple pieces of wood to create the neck reduces left-right and individual differences, resulting in greater stability.

This model features a bolt-on neck joint.

The pickups are MEC J/J Pickups, and the preamp is a MEC 2-band active EQ.

This bass features the brand's signature MEC pickups, resulting in a clear output.

You'll find the smooth attack characteristic of Warwick to be very pleasing.

The sound is characterized by its core tone while containing many beautiful harmonics.

It has a balanced volume from low to high frequencies, and this excellent balance is very appealing.

This is why Warwick basses are so highly valued in ensembles of all genres.

The frets have approximately 90% remaining.

The truss rod is in good condition.

The neck is in good condition.

item.title: Spector Euro Bantam 5 - Trans Cherry Gloss — $1,748.05
item.description:
$1,748.05 · Very Good · Bass Direct

Continuing a 45-year legacy of innovation and top-notch craftsmanship, Spector’s Bantam 5 is a medium-scale powerhouse that packs a serious punch. Like it’s 4-string counterpart, the Bantam 5 provides players with a shorter-scale instrument that does not sacrifice on build quality, playability, or tone. With a 32” scale length and a carefully chambered body, the Bantam 5 is lightweight and extremely comfortable to play. Bantam 5 basses feature the classic NS body style, which is fully carved and contoured to the player’s body using chambered European Alder and a gorgeous figured Maple top. The deep-inset bolt-on neck ensures the utmost resonance and projection, while providing an effortless playing experience with its perfectly carved neck profile and fast 22 fret Rosewood fingerboard. The Bantam 5 is loaded with two EMG 40DC pickups, as well as an EMG BTC active EQ circuit, allowing for maximum versatility when paired with your favorite pedals and amps.

Presented in fantastic condition and supplied with original gig bag.

item.title: 【used】Anaconda Basses / Ultra J5 Essence - Island Blue 2023 4.175kg #23000014V2【GIB Yokohama】【price included SHIPPING & IMPORT TAX & VAT】 — $2,390
item.description:
$2,390 · Very Good · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

Anaconda Basses is a UK-based bass brand that has gained exceptional recognition in recent years, particularly in Europe and North America.

The Ultra J4 Essence, released around 2019, won an award from the prestigious bass media outlet (Bass Guitar Magazine). Following this success, the Ultra J5 Essence and Ultra J6 Essence were released in 2020.

In 2023, all models were completely redesigned to achieve even higher quality.

The body is made of alder, and the neck and fingerboard are made of maple.

The scale length is 34.5 inches, similar to the F-Bass BN5.

A scale length 0.5 inches longer than usual is used, particularly to achieve tightness in the low B string, resulting in a less loose nuance.

Yet, the G string doesn't become too tight, maintaining a flexible nuance.

The slightly thicker C-shaped neck is designed to become a flatter D-shape as you move up to the higher frets.

This allows for smooth changes in thumb position, and the shaping is organic and meaningful, rather than simply being uniform.

The neck incorporates two carbon fiber rods for increased strength.

While a neck that's too strong can sometimes cause the low B and E strings to become unruly, this seems to be resolved by the scale length.

The pickups, named AC5-TN, are an original design.

They offer a moderate amount of compression while maintaining a wide range and rich midrange.

The hum-canceling design and low noise are also major advantages.

The preamp paired with the AC5-TN pickups is the original AC3-TZ Active 3-Band Preamp.

Controls include Volume/Passive Tone, Balancer, Middle (push-pull frequency band switching 250/800Hz), Treble/Bass.

This preamp has a very unique and high-quality character. This bass delivers the expansive low end, crisp midrange, and punchy treble that bassists demand.

It's well-designed overall, exhibiting superior performance at a high level.

The manufacturer, including its manufacturing methods and materials, has a next-generation feel.

This model is highly regarded worldwide; please try it out.

The frets have approximately 80% remaining.

The truss rod is in good condition.

The neck is in good condition.

The body and paintwork show scattered minor dents and scratches, with one dent of approximately 10.0mm each on the top and back of the body.

The electronics are in good condition.

No obvious modifications were observed.

Controls: Master Volume/Passive Tone, Balancer, Middle (Pull-Middle Frequency), Treble/Bass, Active/Passive Switch, Preamp Gain (Inside Cavity)
Nut Width: 48.3mm
Bridge String Spacing: 19.0mm
Scale Length: 34.5mm
Year of Manufacture: 2023
Country of Manufacture: CHINA

During setup, we use D'addario EXL strings, highly trusted by professional musicians.

Characterized by a powerful attack and rich harmonics, these strings truly deserve to be called a standard.

These high-quality strings maximize the performance and character of the instrument, so please enjoy them as well.

GIB craftsmen will adjust the instrument for easy playability before delivery.

item.title: Jackson JS2 Spectra Bass IV Quicksilver E-Bassgitarre — $249.17
item.description:
$249.17 · Brand New · Musikhaus Korn e.K.

Jackson JS2 Spectra Bass IV Quicksilver E-Bassgitarre – Moderner Look, massiver Sound Einleitung

Sind Sie auf der Suche nach einem leistungsstarken, stylischen und vielseitigen E-Bass, der sowohl im Proberaum als auch auf der Bühne überzeugt? Möchten Sie ein Instrument, das sich flexibel an verschiedene Musikstile anpasst und gleichzeitig durch Spielkomfort und modernes Design punktet? Dann ist die Jackson JS2 Spectra Bass IV Quicksilver E-Bassgitarre die perfekte Wahl. Mit ihrer cleveren Korpusform, hochwertigen Komponenten und innovativer Elektronik bietet diese Bassgitarre ein überragendes Spielerlebnis für alle, die mehr wollen – zum absolut fairen Preis.

Hersteller: Jackson – Power, Performance und Präzision

Die Marke Jackson steht seit Jahrzehnten für durchdachte Instrumente mit einer klaren Ausrichtung auf Spielbarkeit, Sound und Stabilität. Ob für Metal, Rock, Funk oder experimentelle Stile – Jackson-Bässe und -Gitarren sind für Musiker gemacht, die keine Kompromisse eingehen. Die JS-Serie verbindet dieses Qualitätsversprechen mit einem attraktiven Preis-Leistungs-Verhältnis und richtet sich an Einsteiger, Fortgeschrittene und Bühnenprofis gleichermaßen. Der Jackson JS2 Spectra Bass IV Quicksilver zeigt, wie viel moderne Bassbaukunst heute auch im Einstiegssegment leisten kann.

Technische Details – Vielseitigkeit trifft Ergonomie

Der Jackson JS2 Spectra Bass setzt mit seiner markanten Offset-Korpusform ein visuelles Statement. Der Korpus aus Pappelholz sorgt dabei nicht nur für ein ausgewogenes Klangbild, sondern auch für ein angenehm niedriges Gesamtgewicht – ein echter Vorteil bei längeren Spielsessions oder Live-Auftritten. Das verlängerte obere Horn wurde so designt, dass es die Position des Gurtknopfs optimiert, um Halslastigkeit zu vermeiden. Das Ergebnis: perfekte Balance, mehr Komfort und weniger Ermüdung beim Spielen.

Das Finish in Quicksilver unterstreicht den modernen Charakter des Instruments und verleiht ihm eine stilvolle, futuristische Optik, die sich nahtlos in jedes Setup einfügt – egal ob auf der Bühne oder im Studio.

Hals & Griffbrett – Schnell, stabil und präzise

Der geschraubte Ahornhals mit Scarf Joint (Schrägverbindung) liefert nicht nur hohe Stabilität, sondern auch ausgezeichnetes Sustain. Das schlanke Speed-Halsprofil ermöglicht schnelle Spielbewegungen, ideal für komplexe Basslines oder genreübergreifende Technik-Experimente. Das 12-Zoll-Radius-Griffbrett aus Lorbeerholz bietet die perfekte Mischung aus komfortabler Bespielbarkeit und Kontrolle über alle 24 Jumbo-Bünde hinweg. Dank der weißen Punkt-Inlays und Seitendots behalten Sie auch auf dunklen Bühnen stets die Übersicht.

Elektronik – Flexibel und durchdacht

In Sachen Tonabnehmer setzt Jackson beim JS2 Spectra Bass IV auf eine bewährte Kombination aus einem P-Style-Tonabnehmer in Halsposition und einem J-Style-Tonabnehmer an der Brücke – beide aus eigener Produktion. Diese Konfiguration eröffnet ein breites Klangspektrum: Vom satten, warmen Bassfundament bis hin zu klaren, durchsetzungsstarken Mitten ist alles möglich. Ideal für Funk, Rock, Pop oder härtere Genres wie Metal.

Über den Blend-Regler lassen sich die beiden Tonabnehmer stufenlos mischen – perfekt für individuelle Klanggestaltung. Zusätzlich bietet der Bass eine aktive Bass-Boost-Schaltung, die über einen eigenen Regler steuerbar ist. So lassen sich in dichten Mixes oder live zusätzliche Tiefenreserven aktivieren. Sollte der Akku einmal leer sein, sorgt der Push/Pull-Lautstärkeregler für Sicherheit: Ein einfacher Zug deaktiviert die aktive Schaltung und schaltet in den passiven Modus um – ohne Tonausfall, ohne Unterbrechung.

Hardware – Stabil, zuverlässig und bühnentauglich

Die Jackson Hardtail-Brücke sorgt für präzise Intonation, erhöhte Sustain-Eigenschaften und eine zuverlässige Stimmung – auch bei intensiver Spielweise. Die versiegelten Die-Cast-Mechaniken bieten reibungsloses Stimmen und langfristige Haltbarkeit. Schwarze Hardware rundet das moderne Design ab und macht den Bass nicht nur funktional, sondern auch optisch zu einem Highlight.

Warum der Jackson JS2 Spectra Bass IV Quicksilver die perfekte Wahl ist

Mit seinem durchdachten Design, seiner klanglichen Flexibilität und seiner robusten Bauweise ist der Jackson JS2 Spectra Bass IV Quicksilver ein idealer Partner für Einsteiger, Fortgeschrittene und Bühnenmusiker. Egal ob du groovst, solierst oder einfach einen soliden Fundament-Sound lieferst – dieser Bass liefert. Das schlanke Halsprofil, die aktive/passive Elektronik und die ausgewogene Korpusform zeigen, wie viel moderne Technik und Spielkomfort in einem erschwinglichen Instrument stecken können.

Wenn du einen Bass suchst, der sowohl für Proberaum, Bühne als auch Studio geeignet ist, wirst du beim JS2 Spectra fündig. Besonders überzeugend ist das Sicherheits-Feature der umschaltbaren Elektronik – ein echtes Plus für alle, die live auf Nummer sicher gehen wollen.

Für welche Musikrichtungen ist der Bass geeignet?

Dank der flexiblen PJ-Tonabnehmerkonfiguration, des Bass-Boosts und der aktiven/passiven Schaltung eignet sich der Jackson JS2 Spectra Bass IV für nahezu jedes Genre: Funk, Rock, Pop, Alternative, Metal oder Gospel – alles ist möglich. Auch als Zweitbass oder Reisebass für erfahrene Musiker ist er eine hervorragende Wahl, da er Vielseitigkeit und Komfort vereint.

Features:
  • 4-saitiger E-Bass mit ausgewogener Offset-Korpusform
  • Korpusmaterial: Pappel – leicht und resonant
  • Finish: Quicksilver – modern und elegant
  • Geschraubter Ahornhals mit Scarf Joint für Stabilität
  • Griffbrett: Lorbeer mit 12"-Radius
  • 24 Jumbo-Bünde für erweiterten Tonumfang
  • Weiße Punkt-Inlays und Seitendots zur Orientierung
  • Jackson P-Style Hals-Pickup & J-Style Bridge-Pickup
  • Blend-Regler zur stufenlosen Pickup-Mischung
  • Lautstärkeregler mit Push/Pull für aktiven/passiven Betrieb
  • Aktiver Bass-Boost (nur im aktiven Modus)
  • Jackson Hardtail-Stegeinheit für hohe Stimmstabilität
  • Versiegelte Druckguss-Mechaniken
  • Schwarze Hardware für professionellen Look
  • Saiten: Vernickelter Stahl (.045–.105)

item.title: Rickenbacker 4003S 2023 - Jetglo — $2,485.96
item.description:
$2,485.96 · Excellent · Bass Direct

This traditional Rickenbacker bass style with dot inlays and no binding was prized by Paul McCartney and Chris Squire when they got their first 4001S models in 1964. Players cherish its traditional look and rounded edges that many claim make it more comfortable than the 4003. The updated 4003S version comes with the improved double-action trussrod system. Of course, the famous solid bass tones, ringing sustain and treble punch we’re known for make it a dream. It’s no wonder we’ve received a steady demand of requests to bring this back. This design was once exclusive to the British distributor Rose Morris in the six-ties. Iconic curves of maple form the body with a traditional Rosewood fingerboard. Dot inlays and no binding make this stand out from the standard 4003.

Presented in fantastic condition and supplied with its original hardcase.

item.title: 【used】Ibanez / SRFF806 4.390kg #I160403221【GIB Yokohama】【price included SHIPPING & IMPORT TAX & VAT】 — $1,265
item.description:
$1,265 · Very Good · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

Ibanez is a leading Japanese guitar brand, globally renowned alongside Fender and Gibson.

The SR series is a long-running series, highly regarded worldwide for its well-balanced combination of a slim body and neck with powerful electronics.

This particular instrument is the SRFF806, a 6-string bass with fanned frets that stands out even among the SR series.

The body is made of ash, and the fingerboard is rosewood.

The neck is a laminate of jatoba and bubinga.

This technique of laminating multiple woods together to create the neck reduces left-right and individual differences, resulting in greater stability.

The pickups are Bartolini BH1, and the preamp is original.

The sound produced by the Ibanez custom preamp and Bartolini BH1 pickups is characterized by a tight low end and wide range.

The sound is balanced by the rich, full midrange typical of Bartolini pickups, creating a pleasingly resonant sound.

The ultra-long-selling SR shape features a three-dimensional curved body, and combined with an incredibly slim neck for a 6-string guitar, it offers unparalleled playability.

This guitar showcases Ibanez's high level of technical skill, seamlessly implementing such a new structure.

The frets have approximately 70% remaining.

The truss rod is in good condition.

The neck is in good condition.

The body and paintwork show some minor scratches, but no noticeable damage.

The electronics are in good condition.

The strap pins have been replaced with Jim Dunlop ones.

$1,078 · Good · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

Fender is, of course, the number one brand of electric guitars and basses.

It's loved by countless top artists, including Jaco Pastorious, Marcus Miller, and Geddy Lee. (Titles omitted)

This model is a Mustang Bass from the Mexican-made Player series, offering excellent value for money.

The body is made of alder, the neck is maple, and the fingerboard is pau ferro.

The neck pickup is a Vintage-Style Split Single-Coil Precision Bass, and the bridge pickup is a Vintage-Style Single-Coil Jazz Bass.

The PJ layout delivers a powerful low end while maintaining a well-defined sound.

It features a 30-inch short scale.

While this might raise concerns about a lack of bass, in reality, the increased string amplitude compared to a long scale results in a fat and distinctive sound.

Combined with its easy-to-handle size, this is a bass that you'll want to keep playing.

item.title: Meridian 2019 Horizon 5 Electric Bass Guitar Exotic Wood Made in Italy w/Soft case — $2,100
item.description:
$2,100 · Good · Anne's Guitar Shop

The Meridian Bass Guitar is entirely handmade in our workshop in Avezzano, Italy.

The single-cutaway design makes it extremely easy to play, even on the higher frets, and the Delano pickups and controls enable a wide range of tones.

The one-piece 1986 vintage hard maple neck and phenolic resin fingerboard produce a very solid sound.


The slim body is finished with a Macassar Ebony top and light ash back, and is highly acclaimed for its responsiveness and ease of playability.

Controls include Volume, Balance, Treble, Mid, Bass, and Mini SW (active/passive).

Because it's handmade, this is a rare model that rarely comes on the market.

There are no noticeable scratches and it's in excellent condition.


Although this is a very important part, the neck will bow inward even when the truss rod is tightened to its limit. To straighten the neck, you'll need to take it to a repair shop for ironing and fingerboard adjustment. This is why we're offering it at such a low price, so please take this into consideration before purchasing.

If you'd like more information about the condition of the neck, please send us a message and we'll answer.

The current string gauges are 040, 060, 075, 095, and 118.

With the strings tuned, the gap between the frets and the strings at the widest point is about 2-3mm.

80% of the frets remain.

The transmission system is good, and the sound quality is excellent.

Net weight: 3430g (7.561856lb)

Comes with a soft case and certificate

(Shipping)

We always ship with a tracking number, so you can order with confidence. You can track your delivery at any time.

We only ship to the address registered on Reverb. If you would like your order shipped to a different address, please update your address on Reverb before placing your order.

We ship Monday through Friday. We cannot ship on weekends because carriers are closed.

We do not mark items below their actual value or mark them as "gifts." This practice is prohibited by Japanese, U.S., and international government regulations.

If your address is in a remote area designated by the carrier, you will need to pay additional shipping fees through PayPal

(Importer Obligations)

Import duties, taxes, and fees are not included in the item price or shipping cost. These charges are the buyer's responsibility. Please check with your country's customs office regarding these additional costs before purchasing.


Thank you for your understanding.

item.title: Spector Euro 5 Classic 2024 - Solid White — $2,485.96
item.description:
$2,485.96 · Excellent · Bass Direct

The Euro Classic series takes inspiration from the Spector basses that took the world by storm in the 1980’s. With their unique silhouette and unmistakable sound, these basses became the choice of the most recognizable bass players of their era. Euro Classic basses feature weight-relieved North American Maple body wings, three-piece maple neck-thru construction, a rosewood fingerboard with mother-of-pearl inlays, and professional grade Spector hardware. All models in the Euro Classic family feature a 34” scale length and a complete electronics package from EMG. 4-string models feature the “reverse” P/J pickup configuration, while the 5-strings feature a J/J configuration. When it comes to looks, there are two new distinct finishes to choose from: Metallic Gold and Metallic Blue, each with chrome hardware. Discover the sound that inspired a decade of legendary bass lines with the Euro Classic series.

EMG ELECTRONICS

The Euro Classic Series comes equipped with active EMG pickups and active BTS pre-amp for authentic 80’s tones.

ICONIC SPECTOR DESIGN

Spector’s iconic, three dimensionally carved body design provides unparalleled comfort during performance.

NECK-THRU CONSTRUCTION

Neck-thru construction is a key element of the Spector style and sound, allowing effortless playability and pronounced mid-range.

This bass is presented in fantastic condition and comes with the original padded gig bag.

item.title: 1978 Rickenbacker 4001 - Mapleglo - OHSC — $4,691.40
item.description:
$4,691.40 · Good · Andy Baxter Bass & Guitars Ltd

1978 Rickenbacker 4001 with a Mapleglo finish in good condition. All original and complete except for the missing pickup cover. Serial number: RH4598. (Aug 1978) This Rickenbacker plays & sounds great - an all-round lovely bass.  

There is general wear and tear, including a few dings & scuffs to the body, neck, and headstock, as well as a repaired crack in the pickguard. The fretboard is very clean, and the original frets have plenty of life left in them. 

Fully set up in our workshop with a fresh set of strings and a great playing action. The truss rods work as they should, and the neck is straight. All electronics and hardware are in good working order with no issues. The pots date to 1978.  Weight 9 lbs 6 oz / 4.26 kg. Nut width: 42.5 mm. Depth at 1st fret: 23 mm.

item.title: 【used】Landscape / SWB-STD Antique Violin Color 6.835kg #2106010【GIB Yokohama】【price included SHIPPING & IMPORT TAX & VAT】 — $1,359
item.description:
$1,359 · Good · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

The electric upright bass market is smaller than that of electric basses, and therefore the number of brands is limited.

Among these limited brands, Landscape stands out with a particularly strong presence.

Many musicians worldwide, including Koichi Osamu, John B. Williams, and Eric Revis, use Landscape basses, and their distribution is primarily centered around jazz. (Titles omitted)

This is the SWB-STD, a solid-body model.

The body is made of alder, the neck is maple 3-ply, and the fingerboard is ebony.

It is equipped with two types of pickups: magnetic and piezo.

The Landscape original PB-U magnetic pickup produces an airy sound with a presence that allows you to feel the nuances of string vibration, while the Fishman USA under-transducer outputs a sound primarily focused on a hard midrange that stands out in an ensemble.

These can be freely combined to create various sounds.

The combination of an airy magnetic pickup and a hard piezo pickup offers a wide range of tones, which is a major attraction.

This is a perfect match for those looking for a highly practical electric upright bass for live performances and recordings.

The truss rod is in good condition.

The neck is in good condition.

The body and paintwork have a few minor dents, but overall it is in good condition.

The electronics are in good condition.

No obvious modifications were observed.

Controls: Balancer, Volume, Bass, Treble
Nut Width: 49.0mm
Bridge String Spacing: 27.0mm
Scale Length: 42.5 Inch
Year of Manufacture: Unknown
Country of Manufacture: JAPAN

GIB's craftsmen will adjust the instrument for easy playability before delivery.

Accessories: Soft case

item.title: Mayones Cali 4 2022 - Transparent Custom Colour — $1,601.57
item.description:
$1,601.57 · Very Good · Bass Direct

The Mayones Cali! It might be half the size of our custom series basses, but it carries the same design philosophy & great tone, playability and highest attention to built quality and ergonomy.

Neck-through-body construction (in both wenge and maple versions), beautiful top woods, highest quality fit & finish – no step has been skipped to ensure that Cali are 100% up to Mayones quality you expect.

It has also one additional feature – Cali is a perfect travel companion due to its compact size and built-in headphone amp. It’s easy to carry, pack and set up to play everywhere & every time.

Our goal and intention is to save as much wood as we can in our production pipeline – Cali is built from leftovers of quality woods we use for our full-size instruments, which are too small to use in a regular-sized instrument. It`s available in a limited number of copies per year.

Presented in fantastic condition and supplied in its original gig bag.

item.title: 2005 Rick Turner Electroline Bass - Natural — $2,314.61
item.description:
$2,314.61 · Excellent · Andy Baxter Bass & Guitars Ltd

2005 Rick Turner Electroline Bass - Natural. Excellent + condition with the original hard case. Serial number: 05 2119. Weight: 8 lbs 13.5 oz / 4.02 kg. Nut width: 38 mm. Depth at 1st fret: 20 mm. 

Minor signs of use, including tiny scuffs and a couple of very small dings at the tip of the headstock.  All electronics work as they should. The frets are in great shape, and the bass has been fully set up in our workshop with low action. 

 Endorsed by legends John McVie and Leland Sklar. This one features Piezo pickups for each string as well as 2 traditional Turner magnetic pickups. The Electroline series is designed to be the most flexible solid-body bass in your collection. The key to the Electroline's tone flexibility is the Turner "Reference" piezo pickup system, providing a separate pickup for each string. The sound is so full that many players opt for the piezo-only model, combined with the Thomastik-Infeld bronze-wrapped nylon Acousticore strings. 

•  Neck: Maple with Carbon Fiber, Rosewood fingerboard, 34" scale 
•  Frets: Dunlop Medium
•  Body: Swamp Ash
•  Bridge: Hipshot with Turner Piezo pickups
•  Tuners: Gotoh Style
•  Pickups: Piezo plus blended Turner magnetic pickups
